example_prompt: Create a static NGN virtual account for an existing customer with the customer's BVN, an alphanumeric reference and a one-day expiry.

customer_id.description: Id of an existing Flutterwave customer the account will belong to.

amount.description: Collection amount. Use 0 for static accounts (any amount). Specify a fixed amount for dynamic/fixed-amount accounts.

reference.description: Unique alphanumeric transaction identifier (6 to 42 characters). Must be unique across all your transactions. Reusing returns 409.

currency.description: ISO 4217 currency code (NGN, GHS, EGP, KES, MAD, ZAR).

account_type.description: static (reusable, fixed account number) or dynamic (one-time per transaction).

expiry.description: Account validity in SECONDS (60 to 31,536,000 — i.e. 1 minute to 1 year). NOT an ISO 8601 datetime.

narration.description: Display name when the account resolves (e.g. customer-facing label).

bvn.description: Customer Bank Verification Number (11 digits). Often required for Nigerian (NGN) virtual accounts.

customer_account_number.description: Originating bank account number. Required for EGP/KES.

bank_code.description: Bank code identifier (optional, restricts which bank backs the account).

meta.description: Custom key-value string metadata stored on the virtual account.

trace_id.description: Optional X-Trace-Id header value for request correlation.

idempotency_key.description: Optional X-Idempotency-Key header value. Same key returns the original response.

scenario_key.description: Optional X-Scenario-Key header value (test environment scenario selector).

id.description: Virtual account id. Store this — required to call get/update_virtual_account.

account_number.description: Generated bank account number to communicate to the customer.

account_bank_name.description: Name of the bank backing the virtual account (e.g. WEMA BANK).

note.description: Message providing instructions for the bank transfer.

customer_reference.description: Reference the customer sends as narration when paying.

gotchas: Flutterwave v4 uses OAuth2 client credentials. POST client_id and client_secret as form-encoded body to https://idp.flutterwave.com/realms/flutterwave/protocol/openid-connect/token to obtain an access_token (valid 10 minutes), then use it as Bearer in the Authorization header. The FLUTTERWAVE_CLIENT_CREDENTIALS env var stores base64(client_id:client_secret).
Required body fields are: customer_id, amount, reference, currency, account_type. Missing any one returns 400 REQUEST_NOT_VALID with validation_errors.
`expiry` is an INTEGER number of SECONDS (60 to 31,536,000), not an ISO 8601 timestamp. The response returns `account_expiration_datetime` as ISO 8601 — do not confuse the two.
`account_type` is mandatory: use `static` for a reusable fixed account number, `dynamic` for one-time per transaction. For static accounts pass `amount: 0` to accept any amount.
`reference` must be unique across ALL your transactions and 6–42 alphanumeric characters. Reusing a reference returns 409 RESOURCE_CONFLICT (code 10409).
`bvn` (11 digits, must start with 1–9) is typically required for Nigerian NGN accounts. `customer_account_number` is required when currency is EGP or KES.
customer_id must reference an existing customer — create the customer first via POST /customers (cus_... ids).
Successful creation returns HTTP 201, not 200. Settlement of funds is reported via the charge.completed webhook — wire it up before going live.
